{{External|rs}}{{Has quick guide}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = A Soul's Bane
|number = 102
|image = [[File:A Soul's Bane.png|300px]]
|release = [[3 April]] [[2006]]
|update = A Soul's Bane
|members = Yes
|series = None
|developer = Tim C
}}
'''A Soul's Bane''' is a combat-focused quest focused on helping [[Launa Talvoy]] find her son, who disappeared into a mysterious rift 25 years ago.

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Go east of [[Varrock]], through the gate by the [[Earth Altar]] and just south of there, speak to [[Launa]].
|startmap = 3308,3454
|difficulty = Intermediate
|description = A rift in the ground has opened up releasing all manner of strange monsters into the lands near the Digsite. A sorrowful mother waits at the top of the rift for her long lost son, who has been missing for 25 years. You can journey down into the rift for an adventure full of twisted monsters all created from the mind of a young boy driven to madness through incarceration.
|length = Short
|requirements = The ability to defeat level 45+ enemies.
|items = *[[Rope]]
*A [[weapon]]
|recommended = *{{SCP|Combat|30|link=yes}}
*[[Food]]
*[[Digsite pendant]] or [[Digsite teleport]]
*3-4 [[antidote++]] or [[antipoison]]
*[[Armour]]
*A method of attacking with [[Ranged]] or [[Magic]]
*A fast weapon, such as [[bronze dart]]s
|kills = *7-8 [[angry unicorn]]s, [[angry bear]]s, [[angry giant rat]]s, and/or [[angry goblin]]s
*5 [[fear reaper]]s ''(level 42)''
*6 [[confusion beast]]s ''(level 43)'' — must tank them, cannot kill
*5 [[hopeless creature]]s ''(level 40)''
*3 parts of [[Tolna]] ''(level 46)''
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Not completable, as it requires access to Misthalin.
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
:'''Note:''' This is a combat heavy quest. [[Attack]] pures should be aware that completing the '''rage''' section will award 40 [[Attack]] experience.
===The missing family===
[[File:A Soul's Bane - Launa at the rift.png|thumb|250x250px|Launa at the rift]]
{{Needed|Combat gear: Ranged or Magic is recommended for lower level players, as enemies only have melee offensive stats. Though some may attack at ranged, their damage will be less. Note that in the first room, you must use given melee weapons.}}
[[File:Launa chathead.png|left|frameless|140x140px]]
Head east of [[Varrock]], through the gate by the [[Earth Altar]], and speak to [[Launa]]. The [[Digsite pendant]] teleport will place you slightly south-east of the entrance. Alternatively, take a [[glider]] to the [[Digsite]].

Launa explains that she is looking for her son, Tolna, as well as husband, who went down into the rift to find him. Offer to help her find them, then use a [[rope]] on the [[Rift (Tolna's rift)|rift]] and climb down to start a cutscene. 

Be careful not to click during this cutscene or subsequent ones, else you'll teleport out and be forced to go through them again.
{{Clear}}

===Tolna's rift===
{{Instance}}

The rift contains four combat rooms, as well as a final boss room at the end. After completing a room, players may choose to leave the rift to get food or supplies, with their progress saved. Any incomplete rooms will be restarted, however. Logging out will also cause the player to leave the rift.

Be aware that the entire quest is [[instance]]d. On [[death]], all unprotected items will be moved to a [[grave]] outside the rift, and any items left on the ground will be lost. '''Do not drop anything''' you'll need later on.

====Rage====
{{Cquote2|''"<big>Why should I, Tolna, be trapped in such a wretched place? Arrghh, It's not fair! Feel my ANGER!</big>"''}}[[File:Weapon rack (Tolna's rift) (Full).png|left|frameless|width=104x104]]
[[File:A Soul's Bane - maximum outrage.gif|thumb|300x300px|The player, outraged, kills all the remaining monsters.]]
''This room represents [[Tolna]]'s anger by frustrating players, as they can only hit monsters with certain weapons.''<br>

The rage room has four types of monsters, which must be killed to fill a rage meter. When entering this room, players will see a [[weapon rack (Tolna's rift)|weapon rack]], which contains four types of weapons. Each of the weapons only work on a specific monster, and will deal normal damage multiplied by 10 when correctly chosen.To defeat the monster, unwield your weapon and shield, and select one of the weapons from the rack and wield it. Only one weapon can be taken at a time. Once you have the weapon, kill the monster it can damage, based on the table below:
{| class="wikitable"
|+
!Monster
!Monster name
!Weapon
!Weapon name
|-
|[[File:Angry unicorn.png|frameless|width=100x100]]
|[[Angry unicorn]]
|[[File:Anger sword detail.png|frameless|width=100x100]]
|[[Anger sword]]
|-
|[[File:Angry bear (level 40).png|frameless|width=100x100]]
|[[Angry bear]]
|[[File:Anger spear detail.png|frameless|width=100x100]]
|[[Anger spear]]
|-
|[[File:Angry giant rat (level 45, 1).png|frameless|width=139x139]]
|[[Angry giant rat]]
|[[File:Anger mace detail.png|frameless|width=102x102]]
|[[Anger mace]]
|-
|[[File:Angry goblin.png|frameless|width=132x132]]
|[[Angry goblin]]
|[[File:Anger battleaxe detail.png|frameless|width=100x100]]
|[[Anger battleaxe]]
|}
[[File:A Soul's Bane - Tolna leaves home.png|thumb|250x250px|Tolna leaves the streets of Varrock in search of a more lucrative life.]]

Players will do large amounts of damage on these creatures, but no experience will be gained. Each creature killed will fill the "rage level" bar at the top of the screen. After several kills of the player's choice of angry creatures, the bar will reach maximum and the player will yell, spin, and kill all remaining monsters in the room. This grants the player 40 Attack experience.

A cutscene will then play, in which Tolna leaves his mother for a more lucrative life. Players can then exit out of the rage room near the east end of the cave, and proceed to the next room.
<br>
====Fear====
{{Cquote2|''"<big>It's so quiet and dark. I FEAR I may never escape this horrid place. Mum, Dad, help me!</big>"''}}[[File:Fear reaper (Level 42).png|left|frameless|width=141x141]][[File:A Soul's Bane - dark holes.png|thumb|300x300px|You must look into the dark holes. Prepare for a small shock.]]''This room represents [[Tolna]]'s fear, giving players a frightening jolt when they don't know whether or not a [[fear reaper]] will attack.''<br>

In this room, you will find multiple [[Dark hole (A Soul's Bane)|dark holes]] lining the outer walls. One of these holes is too dark and frightening to search, while the others have a chance of triggering a 'jumpscare' and summoning a [[fear reaper]]. You will have to kill five of these in order to progress.  Which hole that spawns a [[fear reaper]] is random and changes after each kill, requiring you to move around the room and search different holes after each encounter. Depending on your luck, you might need to circle the room up to five times to get defeat all five reapers. The creatures are large and only attack with melee, allowing them to be easily safespotted.
[[File:A Soul's Bane lit up hole.png|thumb|alt=Picture of the hole for the player to proceed.|width=120x120|Lit up hole to the west]]
<br>
{| class="wikitable"
|+After each kill your character is less afraid.
!Kill count
!Message
|-
|1
|I don't like looking in these holes but I must!
|-
|2
|I don't feel quite so afraid now.
|-
|3
|These holes aren't that spooky.
|-
|4
|This isn't so scary after all!
|}
After the final kill, your character will remark "Phew, I don't feel so scared now. This feels like the figment of someone's imagination!", and a cutscene will be triggered. Following the cutscene, the hole to the west will be lit up, and you will be able to proceed. <br>
====Confusion====
{{Cquote2|''"<big>Where am I, How long have I been here? Am I still the person I once was? Oh why am I so CONFUSED?</big>"''}}[[File:Confusion beast.png|left|frameless|width=100x100]]''This room represents [[Tolna]]'s confusion, flabbergasting the player because some doors lead nowhere and some beasts can't be hurt. ''

[[File:A Soul's Bane - confusion beasts.png|thumb|300x300px|If you're doing 0 damage, then the creature is an illusion.]]

{{Bug|If you don't see any monsters when you enter this room, simply log out and log back in.}}

In this room, there are six [[confusing door]]s. There are also five [[confusion beast]]s, but only one of them is real at the time; the others are illusions, and cannot be damaged (although they will still grant [[combat]] [[experience]]). 

:'''Tip''': On the [[RuneLite]] client, it is possible to color each beast with external plugins. When you find the right one, that same color will be the correct beast all 6 times.

The illusions will disappear after you hit eight zeroes on them; players can simply keep attacking the beasts until they disappear, or disengage by running to the opposite corner and then attacking another beast.

Once you kill the real confusion, one of the confusing doors will vanish, and the rest of the beasts will respawn. Repeat the previous process four more times, until only one door remains, at which point a cutscene will trigger.

[[Ranged]] or [[Magic]] is best for this step, as the beasts attack from a distance. When using [[melee]], they have a chance to inflict [[poison]], which can be negated by attacking with a long-distance weapon. [[Throwing knife|Throwing knives]], [[multi-hit weapons]], or other fast weapons are helpful for speedy kills on the illusions.

Illusions will chase you after attacking, and will keep you from attacking any of the other monsters while you are still in combat.

You will see a cutscene after the last kill. After that, you can proceed through the final Confusing Door.

====Hopelessness====
[[File:A Soul's Bane - the hope bridge.png|thumb|300x300px|The hope bridge that appears after killing all the hopeless creatures.]]
{{Cquote2|''"<big>What's the point? No-One will ever help me. I'm not worth anything, will anyone ever combat my HOPELESSNESS?</big>"''}}
[[File:Hopeless creature.png|left|frameless|width=100x100]]
''This room represents [[Tolna]]'s hopelessness — players must not give up when the creatures reappear after dying.''

In this room are five [[hopeless creature]]s, which need to be killed three times each to fully die. Be careful of eating, as the monster's health will be restored. Additionally, if they are poisoned, the poison effect is lost when they reach their next phase. The creatures can use melee on their second and third forms, but will default to ranged after some time if they cannot reach the player. It is recommended to kill them from a distance as their ranged attacks are much weaker.

Once they have all died, the [[Hope Bridge|Bridge of Hope]] will appear. After crossing the bridge, go through the exit, which will trigger a short cutscene.

====Tolna====
[[File:A Soul's Bane - Brana confronts Tolan.png|thumb|300x300px|Tolna's father, Brana, confronts his son.]]
''The final room contains [[Tolna]], a three-headed beast which must be slain in order to revert him back to a human.''

The final room is a [[multicombat]] zone, and all three heads will attack at once. They use a ranged attack from a distance, and the northern head will very rapidly melee when up close. They are not very strong, but they attack quickly and can [[poison]], so players should bring an [[antipoison]] and a decent amount of food. The three heads are very easy to beat with [[Ranged]] or [[Magic]], although be warned that if using range without an [[Ava's device|accumulator]], player will not be able to retrieve their arrows.

After killing each head, Tolna will become human again. Talk to Tolna, and he will take you back to the surface. Talk to him again to finish the quest.

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

:'''Tip:''' Re-enter the dungeon right after completing the quest to complete a medium [[Varrock Diary]] task.''

{{Clear}}

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = A Soul's Bane
|image = [[File:A Soul's Bane reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 1
|rewards = 
*{{SCP|Defence|500}} [[Defence]] [[experience]]
*{{SCP|Hitpoints|500}} [[Hitpoints]] experience
*500 {{plink|Coins}}
*Access to [[Tolna's rift]] for combat training
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of A Soul's Bane is required for the following:
*[[Medium Varrock Diary]] - Enter the Tolna dungeon after completing the quest.

==Transcript==
{{Hastranscript|quest}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 28 April 2016
|update = Calling Pets & Deadman Death
|poll = 
|change = The Confusion beasts from A Soul's Bane quest are now immune to venom.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
*The map of the final room resembles a brain, possibly representing Tolna's troubled mind as the player fights him to bring things back to normal.
*The music track "[[Fear and Loathing]]" is likely a reference to the author {{wp|Hunter S. Thompson}}, who used the phrase in the title of several of his works. The quest's themes of psychological descent and emotional manifestation may have taken inspiration from his writing.
*The house in the first flashback when Tolna leaves home is the same house the player helps fix up for [[Old Man Yarlo]] in [[Daddy's Home]].

{{A Soul's Bane}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Soul's Bane, A}}
